title: Demo: fix FIXED_INCOME price in DefaultPriceBook from $0.05 to par-equivalent (~100)
description: A government bond does not price at $0.05. Currently DefaultPriceBook.priceFor(FIXED_INCOME) returns $0.05, meaning a $1M UST-10Y notional books as 20,000,000 units. Any rates trader calls this out in 30 seconds and the fixed-income book becomes unusable in the demo. Change to par-equivalent (~98.50 or 100.00) so a $1M notional derives ~10,000 units. One-line fix but kept as its own issue because it blocks the rates trader audience.
design: Files: demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/DefaultPriceBook.kt. May be subsumed by the per-instrument SimulatedPriceBook work, but ship this separately if the per-instrument work is delayed.
acceptance_criteria: Unit test verifies FIXED_INCOME price is in the [95, 105] range. A booked $1M UST trade results in a quantity in the [9000, 11000] unit range.

title: Demo: replace static DefaultPriceBook with realistic per-instrument prices + random walk
description: The single most credibility-destroying artifact in the demo. DefaultPriceBook currently returns fixed per-asset-class spots ($100 EQUITY, $1.10 FX, $0.05 FIXED_INCOME, $75 COMMODITY, $50 DERIVATIVE). AAPL trades at $100 all day every day. Any front-office PM clocks this as synthetic in under 60 seconds. Replace with a SimulatedPriceBook (in-memory, alongside DefaultPriceBook) seeded with realistic per-instrument levels (AAPL ~185, MSFT ~420, NVDA ~880, EURUSD 1.085, etc.) and a small Gaussian random walk (~±0.3% per tick). Change is confined to demo-orchestrator. After fix: trade quantities make sense, MTM P&L actually moves between ticks.
design: Files: demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/DefaultPriceBook.kt, SimulatedTraderJob.kt. Keep DefaultPriceBook as the fallback; wire SimulatedPriceBook behind a config flag (default-on in DEMO_MODE).
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests verify per-instrument seed prices match expected levels; tests verify successive ticks produce different prices within configured drift bounds. Live demo: positions blotter shows realistic prices, unrealized P&L moves between ticks.

title: Phase 3.4: verify firm aggregate populates after bootstrap
description:
New gateway acceptance test gateway/.../routes/CrossBookVaRFirmAfterBootstrapAcceptanceTest.kt. Against a real risk-orchestrator with VaR results seeded for all 8 books, assert GET /api/v1/risk/var/cross-book/firm returns 200 with:
- isPartial == false
- missingBooks empty
- non-zero positive firm VaR within sanity range $50,000 < firmVaR < $20,000,000 (data-analyst review).

If this fails the bootstrap was not sufficient — add a follow-up POST /api/v1/risk/var/cross-book to DemoVaRBootstrapJob in a separate commit and document the decision in the test.
acceptance_criteria: ./gradlew :gateway:acceptanceTest --tests "*CrossBookVaRFirmAfterBootstrapAcceptanceTest"

title: Phase 3.3: wire bootstrap into startup + /demo/bootstrap-status endpoint
description:
In demo-orchestrator/.../Application.kt, after seed phase and before scheduled jobs register, invoke DemoVaRBootstrapJob.runOnce() in a coroutine (non-blocking so HTTP binds promptly).

Expose GET /demo/bootstrap-status returning {state: NOT_STARTED|IN_PROGRESS|READY|FAILED, successCount, failureCount} (in-memory, no DB).

Acceptance test brings up demo-orchestrator with real Postgres + recorded-calls fake risk-orchestrator (pattern from SimulatedTradingAcceptanceTest.kt with ConcurrentLinkedQueue<RecordedVaRPost>). Asserts each distinct book ID appears exactly ONCE in recorded calls (not just count==8) and /demo/bootstrap-status transitions NOT_STARTED → IN_PROGRESS → READY within 30s.
acceptance_criteria: ./gradlew :demo-orchestrator:acceptanceTest --tests "*DemoVaRBootstrapAcceptanceTest"

title: Phase 3.2: SOD baseline bootstrap alongside VaR
description: P&L Attribution route returns 412 Precondition Failed without an SOD baseline (data-analyst review). Extend DemoVaRBootstrapJob to call SodBaselineCaptureJob.runOnce() per book after the VaR call succeeds. Unit test: SOD baseline called for every successfully-VaR'd book, NOT for failed books.
acceptance_criteria: ./gradlew :demo-orchestrator:test --tests "*DemoVaRBootstrapJobTest.sod-baseline*"

title: Phase 3.1: DemoVaRBootstrapJob (TDD)
description:
demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/DemoVaRBootstrapJob.kt with runOnce(): BootstrapResult.

Iterates DevDataSeeder.BOOK_HIERARCHY_MAPPINGS.keys (8 books), calls riskOrchestratorClient.calculateVaR(bookId, BOOTSTRAP_PARAMS).

BOOTSTRAP_PARAMS: confidenceLevel=0.95, horizonDays=10, method=PARAMETRIC, valuationDate=today. Overridable via DEMO_BOOTSTRAP_VAR_CONFIDENCE / DEMO_BOOTSTRAP_VAR_HORIZON env vars.

Retry on ConnectException / 5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts, 500ms/1s/2s). No retry on empty-positions failures (terminal — log and classify as EMPTY).

BootstrapResult shape: data class BootstrapResult(successCount: Int, failureCount: Int, failedBooks: List<String>, durationMillis: Long).

Tests (Kotest FunSpec + MockK):
(a) calls VaR for every seeded book exactly once on first run
(b) idempotency: second runOnce() completes cleanly, same successCount
(c) continues after one book throws, records failure in failedBooks
(d) empty book: 400 'Cannot calculate VaR on empty positions list' → classified EMPTY, not RETRYABLE
(e) retry: ConnectException retried up to 3 times then gives up; transient 5xx retried and eventually succeeds.
acceptance_criteria: ./gradlew :demo-orchestrator:test --tests "*DemoVaRBootstrapJobTest"

title: Phase 0: diagnose actual exception path for 5 gateway 500-returning endpoints
description:
Run a focused integration test pointing each of the 5 routes (KRD, factor-risk, margin, jobs/chart, pnl-attribution) at a stub upstream returning HTTP 404 and HTTP 500. Capture which exception type hits Ktor's pipeline. Outcome determines whether Phase 1 fix is client-level (return-type → nullable) or route-level (try/catch).

Source: plans/issues-1.md Phase 0.1. Architect review noted that Application.kt:170 already reflects upstream status codes via UpstreamErrorException, so the 500-source is likely NPE/deserialisation on getChartData's non-nullable return type — not the exception propagation the original audit assumed.
acceptance_criteria: ./gradlew :gateway:test --tests "*UpstreamExceptionPathDiagnosticTest"

title: Demo: add counterparty diversity in DevDataSeeder (multi-counterparty positions, CP risk view)
description: All demo trades currently book against a single implicit counterparty. A credit risk professional reviewing the demo will ask 'how do I see my top-10 counterparty exposures?' and there is nothing to show. Seed 4-6 counterparties in DevDataSeeder (e.g. GS, JPM, MS, CS, BoA, Citi), rotate book trades through them in SimulatedTraderJob, and surface a CP-exposure view in the UI showing concentrations, netting, and CP-by-asset-class breakdown.
design: Files: risk-orchestrator's DevDataSeeder (add CP entities — get architecture sign-off per CLAUDE.md guardrails). demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/SimulatedTraderJob.kt + StrategyTradeRequest dto: add counterparty assignment per trade (round-robin or weighted). ui: new CP-exposure tile or subtab. position-service must accept and store counterparty on trade booking.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance test verifies trades book against 4-6 distinct counterparties. UI CP-exposure view shows non-trivial concentration distribution. Playwright E2E.

title: Demo: bootstrap splash screen — gate UI on /demo/bootstrap-status=READY
description: If a viewer clicks 'Risk' within the first 30-60 seconds of demo startup, they see empty data while DemoVaRBootstrapJob is still IN_PROGRESS. Add a splash overlay in the UI that polls GET /demo/bootstrap-status (already exposed by demo-orchestrator) and shows a 'Demo environment initializing… (N of 8 books ready)' banner until state=READY. Dismiss automatically.
design: Files: ui/src/components/DemoBootstrapGate.tsx (new), wrap Risk / Positions / P&L / Regulatory routes in this gate. Poll every 2s with backoff. Show per-book progress from the bootstrap-status response payload (successCount, failureCount, failedBooks). Only active when demo-orchestrator returns 200 (i.e. running in DEMO_MODE).
acceptance_criteria: Vitest unit test for the gate component. Playwright E2E in ui/e2e/ verifies the splash renders before bootstrap completes and dismisses once state=READY. Acceptance test confirms gate disables itself when demo-orchestrator is not deployed (non-demo environments).

title: Demo: surface a stress-scenario result (e.g. +100bps rates shock) on Risk overview
description: A CRO will ask 'what happens to this portfolio if rates spike 100bps?' within the first few minutes of looking at the Risk view. Risk-engine has Monte Carlo and stress-testing capability. Surface at least one canned stress-scenario result on the Risk overview — even a static daily result — so the CRO sees the full risk story (VaR + limits + stress) and not just the VaR number.
design: Files: risk-orchestrator (verify it can request stress scenarios from risk-engine and persist results), demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/ (new StressScenarioSeedJob that fires a canned stress on bootstrap + daily). Canned scenarios: '+100bps parallel shift', 'SPX -5% / vol +5pts', 'USD +3%'. ui/src/components/Risk overview gains a stress-result tile.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance test verifies a stress-scenario result is captured for each book on bootstrap. Risk overview UI shows the canned scenario card with non-zero P&L impact. Playwright E2E.

title: Demo: add DV01 column to rates-instrument Greek display
description: A fixed-income trader looking at the macro-hedge and fixed-income books expects DV01 (dollar value of a basis point) as the primary rates risk metric, not just generic Delta. Risk-engine almost certainly already computes DV01 (or PV01) for rates instruments; surface it in the P&L Waterfall and the Risk overview for rates books. Without DV01 visible, a rates trader walks away unconvinced even if the underlying calculation is correct.
design: Files: ui/src/components for P&L Waterfall and Risk overview Greek attribution panel (add DV01 column gated on instrument-type=FIXED_INCOME). Backend: confirm risk-engine surfaces DV01 in the snapshot/greek payload; if not, plumb through risk-orchestrator -> gateway.
acceptance_criteria: UI shows DV01 column for FIXED_INCOME positions in P&L Waterfall and Risk hierarchy views. Vitest unit test for the component. Playwright E2E verifies DV01 column renders and contains non-zero values for UST positions after demo seeds trades.

title: Demo: use realistic option prices in DefaultPriceBook (SPX 5000C ~180, VIX 20C realistic)
description: DefaultPriceBook prices all DERIVATIVE instruments at a flat $50. VIX-OPT-20C at $50 implies VIX is at a panic level — a vol PM will spot this immediately. SPX-OPT-5000C should price around $150-200 given a typical vol environment, not $50. Replace the flat DERIVATIVE price with per-instrument lookups consistent with realistic strikes/IVs (depends on the SPX/VIX reference-data wiring in the partner issue). Use risk-engine's pricer (BS or Heston) at startup to compute fair value of each option seed, store as the initial price, and let the random walk handle subsequent moves.
design: Files: demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/DefaultPriceBook.kt or its successor SimulatedPriceBook. May depend on the per-instrument prices issue and the SPX/VIX reference-data wiring issue — order via bd dep add.
acceptance_criteria: Unit test verifies SPX-OPT-5000C seed price in [120, 250] range and VIX-OPT-20C in a vol-consistent range (typically $1-$5 for 20-strike calls). A vol PM looking at the derivatives-book blotter does not see flat $50 across all options.
close_reason: BS-derived option seeds shipped — SPX 5000C ~, VIX 20C ~.59, both within acceptance bounds. Verified by OptionPriceSeederTest.

title: Demo: wire SPX/VIX options to reference-data with realistic strikes, expiries, and IV
description: Derivatives book currently uses placeholder instrument IDs (SPX-OPT-5000C, ES-FUT-MAR, VIX-OPT-20C) with no structured detail. A vol PM will ask for strike, expiry, and implied vol within the first 60 seconds of looking at the book. Without reference-data integration, you cannot answer. Wire these instruments to reference-data-service with realistic structured fields (SPX 5000-strike Mar/Jun/Sep expiries, VIX 20-strike, ES Mar future) so the option detail panel shows strike, expiry, IV, and option-greeks attribution that a vol PM would actually recognize.
design: Files: reference-data-service (add option-instrument seed entries — get architecture sign-off per CLAUDE.md guardrails before adding new data shape). risk-engine option-pricing wiring (likely already supports BS/Heston for SPX-style options). demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/profile/DemoBookProfiles.kt — replace placeholder ids with real reference-data instrument ids.
acceptance_criteria: UI option detail panel shows strike, expiry, IV, delta, gamma, vega for each derivatives-book instrument. Acceptance test verifies reference-data lookup returns structured fields, not raw strings.

title: Demo: add multiple sub-strategies per book in risk-orchestrator DevDataSeeder
description: Currently SimulatedTraderJob falls back to '{bookId}-default' as the only strategy per book (DefaultStrategyIdResolver). No real fund runs a single strategy per book; a buy-side viewer notices the shallow hierarchy in a 15-minute walkthrough. Add 2-3 sub-strategies per book in risk-orchestrator's DevDataSeeder so the portfolio tree drills down like a real fund structure (e.g. equity-growth -> {core, satellite}; derivatives-book -> {vol-arb, directional, hedge}).
design: Files: risk-orchestrator's DevDataSeeder (cross-module change, get architecture sign-off per CLAUDE.md guardrails before adding new data shape). demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/SimulatedTraderJob.kt — when picking strategy, randomly choose from book.strategies rather than '{bookId}-default'. demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/DefaultStrategyIdResolver.kt — list strategies from server rather than synthesizing a default.
acceptance_criteria: Each demo book has at least 2 strategies in DevDataSeeder. SimulatedTraderJob distributes trades across strategies roughly proportionally. UI portfolio tree shows book -> strategy drill-down with non-trivial position counts under each strategy.

title: Demo: display per-instrument prices in trade blotter UI
description: Even before the random-walk price work lands, the UI should display the seed price per instrument in the trades blotter and positions grid. A viewer who sees 'AAPL 1,850 shares at $100.00' immediately knows something is wrong; a viewer who sees 'AAPL 1,000 shares at $185.20' thinks 'that's close to where AAPL was last year.' The psychology of realistic-looking prices is disproportionate to implementation effort. Blocks on kx-y8g (realistic price work) only for the random-walk view; the column should exist regardless.
design: Files: ui/src/components/TradeBlotter.tsx (price column), ui/src/components/PositionGrid.tsx (last-price column). Surface price from position-service trade record (already persisted) — no new backend work required.
acceptance_criteria: Vitest unit test verifies price column renders. Playwright E2E in ui/e2e/ verifies the column is visible on Trades and Positions tabs and contains non-zero values after the demo seeds trades.

title: Demo: pre-seed a guaranteed limit-breach book for visible early-walkthrough breach event
description: LimitSeedJob sets limits at 80% of current VaR / 70% of delta. With a freshly-bootstrapped portfolio this means breaches only appear after trades accumulate, which can take longer than the available walkthrough time. Designate one book (suggest derivatives-book given its 0.40 trade probability) to have its limit seeded at 50% of initial exposure instead of 80%, guaranteeing a breach within the first 2-3 trade ticks. A visible limit breach in the first few minutes is a powerful credibility signal.
design: Files: demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/LimitSeedJob.kt. Add env var DEMO_BREACH_BOOK (default 'derivatives-book') and DEMO_BREACH_VAR_FACTOR (default 0.50). When seeding limits, if the bookId matches DEMO_BREACH_BOOK, use the tighter factor.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance test: with DEMO_BREACH_BOOK=derivatives-book and trade cadence accelerated, a VAR_95 breach event is recorded for derivatives-book within the first 5 trade ticks of bootstrap completing.

title: Demo: reduce reconciliation break probability from 5% to 0.5-1%
description: SimulatedTraderJob currently posts a deliberately-mismatched prime-broker statement for ~5% of trades (default 0.05). At 90s cadence across 8 books that produces dozens of breaks per trading day, which looks like a system in crisis rather than a system catching edge cases. Real ops break rate target is <0.1%. Reduce DEFAULT_RECONCILIATION_BREAK_PROBABILITY to 0.005-0.01 so we still surface a handful of breaks per day (enough to demo the Reconciliation subtab) but the blotter does not look broken to an ops head.
design: Files: demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/SimulatedTraderJob.kt. Env var DEMO_RECONCILIATION_BREAK_PROBABILITY already plumbed; change DEFAULT_RECONCILIATION_BREAK_PROBABILITY constant. Consider biasing breaks toward derivatives-book (highest churn) so one prominent break shows up rather than many spread thin.
acceptance_criteria: Unit test verifies the default is in [0.005, 0.01]. Manual demo run shows 1-3 breaks per simulated trading hour across all books, not dozens.

title: Demo: add POST /demo/trigger-eod admin endpoint for on-demand EOD cycle
description: EodPromotionJob currently fires only at the configured wall-clock close time (default 16:30 UTC). Live demos rarely happen at 16:30 UTC, so the regulatory cycle (the strongest moment of the demo) is unreachable in a 15-minute walkthrough unless you ran the env the night before. Add a POST /demo/trigger-eod admin route that fires the full EodPromotionJob synchronously: VaR snapshot all 8 books, promote each to OFFICIAL_EOD, let risk-orchestrator publish risk.official-eod Kafka events, which EodCycleObserverJob then catches to run backtests and draft regulatory submissions. The endpoint should return 202 with a correlation id once promotion is dispatched.
design: Files: demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/routes/ (new TriggerEodRoutes.kt), demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/Application.kt (route registration), reuse EodPromotionJob.run(). Auth: behind DEMO_MODE=true only, no public exposure outside demo deployments. Optional follow-up: a corresponding POST /demo/trigger-sod for symmetry.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance test fires POST /demo/trigger-eod, verifies all 8 books receive OFFICIAL_EOD promotions, verifies risk.official-eod Kafka events are observed, verifies EodCycleObserverJob runs the backtest and submission draft. Bypasses wall-clock schedule entirely.

title: Copilot polish §7: demo seeding for saved queries + wiki status flip
description: From plans/ai-copilot-demo-polish.md §7 (7.1–7.2). Verify ai-insights-service canned-mode transcript fixtures cover the 5 saved queries with believable citations + tool_calls (post §5a). Extend thin fixtures. Then flip the v2 chat / morning brief / intraday push / ⌘K rows in docs/wiki/AI-Features.md from 'In flight' to 'Shipped' and add a 'Demo polish' row pointing at this plan. Files: ai-insights-service/src/kinetix_insights/fixtures/chat_transcripts/*.json, ai-insights-service/tests/test_*.py, docs/wiki/AI-Features.md.
close_reason: Wave 2a merged to main. §4 book-reset (2 commits, 34 unit + 8 e2e tests), §7 demo fixtures + wiki flip (2 commits, 53 acceptance tests). 5 new chat-transcript fixtures + AI-Features status table updated.

title: Copilot polish §5b: tool-call reasoning (FRONTEND ToolCallList + wiring)
description: From plans/ai-copilot-demo-polish.md §5 (5.3–5.4). Frontend half: mirror the ToolCall type in ui/src/api/copilot.ts and add a <ToolCallList> component rendering a collapsible <details> 'Show reasoning ({N} tool calls)' below <StreamingNarrative>. Each call shows name, status icon, and a nested <details> for params. Wire into both <StreamingNarrative> and <CommandPalette> chat surfaces. Extend ui/e2e/copilot-chat.spec.ts. Depends on §5a (the ToolCall shape must be agreed). Files: ui/src/api/copilot.ts, ui/src/components/ToolCallList.tsx (new) + .test.tsx, ui/src/components/StreamingNarrative.tsx, ui/src/components/CommandPalette.tsx, ui/e2e/copilot-chat.spec.ts.
close_reason: §5b merged. 5 commits, 16+78 unit tests + Playwright pass. ToolCallList wired via StreamingNarrative's internal state (single source of truth, no DOM dup).

title: Copilot polish §6: 'dashboards remain the source of truth' footnote
description: From plans/ai-copilot-demo-polish.md §6 (6.1). Add a one-line text-xs text-slate-400 footnote 'Dashboards remain the source of truth. The Copilot narrates them, it doesn't replace them.' above the saved-query chips in the ⌘K empty state and at the bottom of <MorningBriefCard>. Never sticky. Vitest covers presence on both surfaces. Marcus: 'When the chatbot's number disagrees with the dashboard, the dashboard wins, and the chatbot says so.' Files: ui/src/components/CommandPalette.tsx + .test.tsx, ui/src/components/MorningBriefCard.tsx + .test.tsx.
close_reason: Wave 1 merged to main. §1 launcher (3 commits, 6 Playwright tests), §2 freshness urgency badges (2 commits, 38 tests), §3 friendly error messages (2 commits, 38 tests), §5a backend ChatChunk.tool_calls (5 commits, 638 tests), §6 source-of-truth footnote (1 commit, 39 tests).

title: Copilot polish §4: book-boundary conversation reset
description: From plans/ai-copilot-demo-polish.md §4 (4.1–4.2). Add a bookId prop to <CommandPalette> in ui/src/components/CommandPalette.tsx. When bookId changes while conversationId/copilotStream/copilotAnswered are non-empty, clear them and show a one-line dismissible banner inside the palette body. Wire bookId={effectiveBookId ?? bookId} from ui/src/App.tsx. Extend ui/e2e/copilot-chat.spec.ts to assert the reset behaviour. Marcus: 'switching books mid-conversation must reset or warn — otherwise it'll cite wrong numbers from the prior context.' Files: ui/src/components/CommandPalette.tsx + .test.tsx, ui/src/App.tsx, ui/e2e/copilot-chat.spec.ts.
close_reason: Wave 2a merged to main. §4 book-reset (2 commits, 34 unit + 8 e2e tests), §7 demo fixtures + wiki flip (2 commits, 53 acceptance tests). 5 new chat-transcript fixtures + AI-Features status table updated.

title: Copilot polish §5a: tool-call reasoning (BACKEND ChatChunk.tool_calls)
description: From plans/ai-copilot-demo-polish.md §5 (5.1–5.2). Backend half: extend ChatChunk (done variant) in ai-insights-service/src/kinetix_insights/chat/models.py with optional tool_calls: list[ToolCall], where ToolCall is a new pydantic model {name, params, status: ok|error|timeout, started_at, completed_at}. Populate tool_calls in ClaudeAgentCopilotChatClient (accumulating SDK tool-use events) and CannedCopilotChatClient (reading extended fixtures). Files: ai-insights-service/src/kinetix_insights/chat/models.py, chat/claude_agent_chat_client.py, chat/canned.py, src/kinetix_insights/fixtures/chat_transcripts/*.json, tests/test_chat_models.py, tests/test_chat_acceptance.py, tests/test_claude_agent_chat_client.py, tests/test_canned_chat_client.py.
close_reason: Wave 1 merged to main. §1 launcher (3 commits, 6 Playwright tests), §2 freshness urgency badges (2 commits, 38 tests), §3 friendly error messages (2 commits, 38 tests), §5a backend ChatChunk.tool_calls (5 commits, 638 tests), §6 source-of-truth footnote (1 commit, 39 tests).

title: Copilot polish §3: friendly error messages for chat
description: From plans/ai-copilot-demo-polish.md §3 (3.1–3.2). Add mapChatErrorCode(code) helper in ui/src/api/copilot.ts that maps CITATION_UNVERIFIABLE / POLICY_VIOLATION / fallback to human-readable {title, body}. Replace the raw errorCode render in ui/src/components/StreamingNarrative.tsx with the mapped title+body in a rose-tinted info panel. Specific copy in the plan's 'Decisions applied' section. Files: ui/src/api/copilot.ts + copilot.test.ts, ui/src/components/StreamingNarrative.tsx + .test.tsx.
close_reason: Wave 1 merged to main. §1 launcher (3 commits, 6 Playwright tests), §2 freshness urgency badges (2 commits, 38 tests), §3 friendly error messages (2 commits, 38 tests), §5a backend ChatChunk.tool_calls (5 commits, 638 tests), §6 source-of-truth footnote (1 commit, 39 tests).

title: Copilot polish §2: freshness urgency badges (red >60s, amber 30–60s)
description: From plans/ai-copilot-demo-polish.md §2 (2.1–2.2). In ui/src/components/CitationList.tsx refactor formatFreshness to return {label, urgency: 'fresh'|'aging'|'stale'} using thresholds ≤30s/≤60s/>60s. Render the badge red (stale) / amber (aging) / neutral (fresh). Mirror urgency on each <CitationFootnote> superscript when aging or stale only — keep neutral citations unintrusive. Marcus: '90-second-old VaR is a lie. Make staleness aggressive.' Files: ui/src/components/CitationList.tsx + .test.tsx, ui/src/components/CitationFootnote.tsx + .test.tsx.
close_reason: Wave 1 merged to main. §1 launcher (3 commits, 6 Playwright tests), §2 freshness urgency badges (2 commits, 38 tests), §3 friendly error messages (2 commits, 38 tests), §5a backend ChatChunk.tool_calls (5 commits, 638 tests), §6 source-of-truth footnote (1 commit, 39 tests).

title: Copilot polish §1: visible discovery affordance (CopilotLauncher in header)
description: From plans/ai-copilot-demo-polish.md §1 (1.1–1.3). Add a <CopilotLauncher> button to the header right cluster in ui/src/App.tsx — Sparkles icon, 'Ask Kinetix' label, dimmed ⌘K chip — that opens the existing <CommandPalette> in copilotMode. Marcus (trader) flagged: today ⌘K is keyboard-only and a demo viewer never finds the AI. Placement: between <ScenarioIndicator> and <RegimeIndicator>. Includes Vitest + Playwright (ui/e2e/copilot-launcher.spec.ts). Files: ui/src/components/CopilotLauncher.tsx (new), ui/src/App.tsx, ui/e2e/copilot-launcher.spec.ts (new).
close_reason: Wave 1 merged to main. §1 launcher (3 commits, 6 Playwright tests), §2 freshness urgency badges (2 commits, 38 tests), §3 friendly error messages (2 commits, 38 tests), §5a backend ChatChunk.tool_calls (5 commits, 638 tests), §6 source-of-truth footnote (1 commit, 39 tests).

title: AI Copilot demo polish: discoverability + Marcus's trust signals
description: Plan at plans/ai-copilot-demo-polish.md. The ai-v2 Copilot is built (74/75 done) but invisible in the UI — ⌘K is keyboard-only. Marcus (senior FX/rates trader) called out: (1) no header affordance, (2) freshness shown as plain text not red-badged, (3) raw error codes leak through, (4) no conversation reset on book switch, (5) no tool-call reasoning panel, (6) no 'dashboard wins' framing. Plan is loop-ready: 7 sections, 13 checkboxes, each with an acceptance command. No new deps, no new services, no CI changes.

title: Phase 1 (revised): handle ConnectException/HttpRequestTimeoutException at StatusPages → 503
acceptance_criteria: ./gradlew :gateway:test --tests "*UpstreamExceptionPathDiagnosticTest"
notes: Architect+QA reviews flagged the wrong layer; diagnostic confirmed. Replaces kx-3ri/chv/bgd/kzu/23z/2sp.

title: Phase 4.3: firm KPI bar shows real numbers (Playwright)
description:
ui/e2e/firm-kpi-bar.spec.ts. Load /, wait for bootstrap banner to disappear (waitForSelector state:'detached'), assert each KPI cell (NAV, UNREALISED P&L, VAR 1D 95%, NET DELTA, NET VEGA) contains a non-zero formatted number matching ^[-$£€]?[\d,]+(\.\d+)?[KMB]?$ and is not $0.00.

Does NOT assert 'no —' (em-dash with tooltip is correct state if data later goes missing — UX review).
acceptance_criteria: cd ui && npx playwright test e2e/firm-kpi-bar.spec.ts

title: Phase 4.2: redeploy + run audit with --assert-clean
description:
Full redeploy (./deploy/redeploy.sh), wait for /health green AND GET /demo/bootstrap-status → READY, then run audit with --assert-clean. Capture report under docs/audits/2026-05-26-issues-1-after.json.

Exit criterion: hard gates all pass. If a hard gate fails after one re-run, escalate as issues-1-followup and STOP — do not silently retry.
acceptance_criteria: node scripts/demo-audit/audit.mjs --base https://kinetixrisk.ai --assert-clean --wait-for-bootstrap
notes:
HANDOFF 2026-05-26: All upstream work done & pushed to origin (commits 8419b070..3330751e). Final verification ready to run.

To execute:
  ./deploy/redeploy.sh
  # wait for: curl -fsk https://kinetixrisk.ai/health and /demo/bootstrap-status returns state=READY
  node scripts/demo-audit/audit.mjs --base https://kinetixrisk.ai --assert-clean --wait-for-bootstrap

Hard gates (per plan): 0 page errors, 0 HTTP 5xx, no $0.00 KPI cells after banner clears, no stuck spinners past 8s.

Capture report under docs/audits/2026-05-26-issues-1-after.json.

If a hard gate fails after one re-run, file as issues-1-followup and STOP — do not silently retry (per plan).

title: Phase 4.1: commit audit harness as scripts/demo-audit/
description:
Move ad-hoc /tmp/kinetix-audit/audit.mjs into scripts/demo-audit/audit.mjs. Single dependency: Playwright (already in ui/).

Hard gates (fail script on violation): (a) 0 page errors, (b) 0 HTTP 5xx, (c) no KPI cell equals $0.00 after bootstrap banner clears, (d) no visible spinner element after 8s of stable network on any tab.

Soft gates (warn but don't fail): row count per tab, per-persona consistency (firm VaR identical across persona switches), per-book varValue > 0.
acceptance_criteria: node scripts/demo-audit/audit.mjs --base https://kinetixrisk.ai --out /tmp/kinetix-audit-ci

title: Phase 2.5.2: Initialising demo data banner during bootstrap
description:
Dismissible banner above tab bar, appears when GET /demo/bootstrap-status returns state != READY, polls every 3s, auto-dismisses on READY. Vitest: render-on-IN_PROGRESS, hide-on-READY, manual-dismiss. Playwright: banner appears on first load, disappears within 15s.

Depends on [[phase-3-3-wire-bootstrap]] (the /demo/bootstrap-status endpoint).
acceptance_criteria: cd ui && npm run test -- BootstrapBanner && cd ui && npx playwright test e2e/bootstrap-banner.spec.ts

title: Phase 2.5.1: Firm KPI bar renders — on missing data, never $0.00
description: When the firm aggregate endpoint returns 404/null, render '—' with title='Calculating…' tooltip. UX review: $0.00 implies a legitimate answer; em-dash implies pending. Vitest: when data prop is null, cells show '—' and never $0.00.
acceptance_criteria: cd ui && npm run test -- FirmKpiBar

title: Phase 2.1: gate AuditLogPanel fetches on auth.initialising
description:
ui/src/components/AuditLogPanel.tsx:115-144 fires two useEffect fetches on mount before DemoAuthProvider sets the persona, causing perpetual 'Verifying chain…' / 'Loading audit events…' on first navigation despite endpoints working.

Fix: pull useAuth(), short-circuit both useEffects with 'if (auth.initialising) return', add auth.initialising to dep arrays. Also change user-visible copy 'Verifying chain…' → 'Loading activity log…' (UX review).

Vitest cases: (a) does not call fetch while initialising; (b) calls fetch exactly once when auth flips from initialising to ready; (c) new copy present, old copy absent.
acceptance_criteria: cd ui && npm run test -- AuditLogPanel

title: Phase 1.5: null-safe GET /api/v1/risk/pnl-attribution/{bookId}
description: gateway/.../routes/SodSnapshotRoutes.kt:67-86 returns 500 when upstream lacks data. Existing null-check on line 81 preserved. Same fix + test pattern as 1.1.
acceptance_criteria: ./gradlew :gateway:acceptanceTest --tests "*SodSnapshotRoutesAcceptanceTest"
close_reason: superseded by kx-9v7 diagnostic — 500s were transient ConnectException/timeouts, not handler bugs. Endpoints already return correct 404 for upstream-404. New ticket created for the actual residual risk (transport exceptions to 503).

title: Phase 1.1: null-safe GET /api/v1/risk/krd/{bookId}
description:
gateway/.../routes/KeyRateDurationRoutes.kt:9-19 returns HTTP 500 when upstream lacks data. Make upstream-404 → gateway-404, upstream-5xx → gateway-503. Fix layer (client vs route) decided by [[diagnostic outcome]].

Tests required (acceptance test class KeyRateDurationRoutesAcceptanceTest):
(a) happy path; (b) upstream-404 → gateway-404; (c) upstream-500 → gateway-503; (d) regression guard: upstream-404 must NOT produce gateway-500; (e) malformed bookId (empty/whitespace) → gateway-400.
acceptance_criteria: ./gradlew :gateway:acceptanceTest --tests "*KeyRateDurationRoutesAcceptanceTest"
close_reason: superseded by kx-9v7 diagnostic — 500s were transient ConnectException/timeouts, not handler bugs. Endpoints already return correct 404 for upstream-404. New ticket created for the actual residual risk (transport exceptions to 503).

title: Live AI in demo: install Claude CLI in ai-insights-service image
description: docker-compose default is DEMO_MODE=false so the factory tries live mode, but the live path fails for two reasons in ai-insights-service/Dockerfile: (1) the runtime image has no `claude` Node CLI (the Python claude-agent-sdk spawns it as a subprocess); (2) the container runs as USER kinetix with no HOME set, while compose mounts ~/.claude:/root/.claude:ro — the SDK reads $HOME/.claude so the mount is effectively ignored. Fix: install Node + @anthropic-ai/claude-code in the runtime image, give the kinetix user a home dir, set HOME=/home/kinetix, and remap the mount to /home/kinetix/.claude in compose and the helm chart.

title: Wire up or remove dead useAlertStream alert WebSocket hook
description: audit-v2 checkbox 11.3 found ui/src/hooks/useAlertStream.ts has full reconnect/backoff logic but is unwired dead code -- the app never opens /ws/alerts. NotificationStrip/NotificationCenter are fed only by a one-shot REST fetch, so alerts raised while the page is open are not shown live. Either wire useAlertStream into the UI for a real live alert stream, or remove the dead hook.

title: Emit risk_calculation_failed_total counter for RiskCalculationFailureRate alert
description: audit-v2 checkbox 10.2 added a RiskCalculationFailureRate alert rule but left it commented out in alert-rules.yml and the Helm chart because no Prometheus metric for failed risk runs exists. VaRCalculationService's failure catch block publishes a RISK_CALCULATION_FAILED audit event but increments no counter. Add a risk_calculation_failed_total Micrometer counter (or a status label on var.calculation.count) in the catch block, then uncomment the alert rule.

title: Surface correlationId in AuditEventResponse DTO
description: audit-v2 plan stored correlationId on audit_events (V13) and its decision says it should be 'surfaced in the API/UI', but the AuditEventResponse route DTO never added the field. ui/src/api/audit.ts types it optional as a forward-compat placeholder. Add correlationId to AuditEventResponse + its mapper so the Activity tab can display it.

title: Update finance/system glossaries to current code
description: docs/glossary/kinetix.md and generic.md were last aligned at commit bdffcd3a (2026-04-29). 723 commits since, incl. new services (ai-insights-service, demo-orchestrator, fix-gateway, load-tests, smoke-tests). Bring both glossaries current.
close_reason: Both glossaries aligned with current code; commit 37d41208 on origin/main

title: Demo: handle market holidays in SimulatedTraderJob (no trading on US holidays)
description: SimulatedTraderJob already skips weekends but does not pause for market holidays (Thanksgiving, Christmas, July 4, etc.). Demoing on a US holiday with trades printing is a minor but visible authenticity gap. Add a US holiday calendar check alongside the existing weekday check.
design: Files: demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/SimulatedTraderJob.kt and SchedulingHelpers.kt. Use a simple static set of US market holidays for the current/next year (no external API). If a holiday calendar utility already exists in common/ or reference-data, prefer reusing it.
acceptance_criteria: Unit test verifies SimulatedTraderJob no-ops on 2026-07-03 (observed July 4), 2026-11-26 (Thanksgiving), 2026-12-25, etc. Production calendar covers at least 12 months forward.

title: Demo: document DEMO_TRADE_CADENCE_SECONDS=30 as the live-demo setting
description: Trade cadence defaults to 90 seconds, which is too slow for a live walkthrough where you want visible blotter activity within 2-3 minutes. The env var DEMO_TRADE_CADENCE_SECONDS already exists in DemoConfig — document 30 as the recommended live-demo setting and 90 as the recommended background-running setting. Add to demo runbook / README.
design: Files: deploy/ (compose or helm values for live-demo profile) — keep 90s as the default for normal runs, override to 30s in a 'live-demo' profile. demo-orchestrator/README.md or a docs/demo/runbook.md if appropriate. Surface in the /demo skill output too.
acceptance_criteria: Documented in the demo runbook / readme that 30 = live-demo, 90 = background. Live-demo compose profile sets DEMO_TRADE_CADENCE_SECONDS=30.

title: Phase 2.2: Playwright regression for Activity tab
description: ui/e2e/activity-tab.spec.ts. Use waitForSelector on [data-testid='audit-event-row'] (not wall-clock 5s timeout). Assert presence only, not count (QA review).
acceptance_criteria: cd ui && npx playwright test e2e/activity-tab.spec.ts

acceptance_criteria: ./gradlew :gateway:acceptanceTest --tests "*KeyRateDuration*" --tests "*FactorRisk*" --tests "*Margin*" --tests "*JobHistory*" --tests "*SodSnapshot*"
